title: draba crassifolia var. parryi
intro: Draba crassifolia var. parryi, also known as Parry's whitlowgrass, is a perennial plant belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to be effective in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Parry's whitlowgrass include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Parry's whitlowgrass can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
